concerns.text: The spec includes a research question and method description in User Story 1 that contradicts the upstream idea. The text states: 'Can large language models be effectively used to generate adversarial examples for evaluating the robustness of text classification models?' and references 'text classification models'. The upstream idea explicitly defines the research question as: 'How do minor input perturbations... affect the functional correctness of code generated by open-source large language models?' This is a fundamental drift redefining the research object (code generation vs. text classification) and the dependent variable (pass@1 vs. classification accuracy).

The reference cited in User Story 1 ('Goodfellow et al., 2014') and the context of 'text classification models' are irrelevant to the upstream idea's focus on LLM code generation robustness. The spec has adopted a template or example text that describes a completely different scientific study, violating the scope fidelity to the 'Evaluating the Robustness of LLM-Generated Code' idea.
